required_inputs: Select one primary reason for seeking help
insurance_or_payment_step: No insurance or payment shown yet
safe_stopping_point: Continue before entering deeper personal or payment details
main_strength: Simple first screen with clear condition options and clear controlled-substance disclaimer
main_friction: Only one primary reason can be selected, which may undercapture overlapping mental-health needs
legion_comparison: Legion allows multi-select conditions, which is stronger for comorbid needs. Talkspace is simpler but less flexible.
notes: Observed options included Anxiety, ADD/ADHD, Depression, Insomnia, Bipolar disorder, Borderline personality disorder, PTSD, OCD, and Something else. Footer stated Talkspace cannot prescribe controlled substances and stimulants.

required_inputs: Answer whether user has ever been prescribed medication for a mental health condition
insurance_or_payment_step: No insurance or payment shown yet
safe_stopping_point: Continue before entering deeper personal or payment details
main_strength: Good clinical routing question early in flow
main_friction: Adds another clinical question before user sees pricing, provider, or booking options
legion_comparison: Legion asks care need in a broader way. Talkspace asks a more medication-specific question earlier.
notes: Observed options were Yes, No, and I'm not sure. User selected I'm not sure during walkthrough. Controlled-substance limitation note appeared again.

required_inputs: Select gender identity
insurance_or_payment_step: No insurance or payment shown yet
safe_stopping_point: Continue before entering deeper personal or payment details
main_strength: May improve provider matching and personalization
main_friction: Adds personal-information friction before pricing, provider visibility, or booking
legion_comparison: Legion did not ask this before provider match in the observed flow. Talkspace collects more personal matching data earlier.
notes: Observed options included Male, Female, Transgender male, Transgender female, Gender queer, Gender variant, Other, and Non-binary.

required_inputs: Enter date of birth
insurance_or_payment_step: No insurance or payment shown yet
safe_stopping_point: Continue before entering payment or final booking
main_strength: Age validation is necessary for care eligibility
main_friction: Talkspace splits simple demographic fields into separate screens, increasing clicks
legion_comparison: Legion collects less demographic data before provider match in the observed flow. Talkspace has more screens before provider visibility.
notes: DOB was requested as a standalone step.

required_inputs: Select state where user lives most of the year
insurance_or_payment_step: No insurance or payment shown yet
safe_stopping_point: Continue before entering payment or final booking
main_strength: Clear licensure routing by state
main_friction: Another separate step before provider or plan visibility
legion_comparison: Legion asked fit and eligibility questions earlier. Talkspace uses standalone state validation before payment and matching.
notes: Observed Massachusetts selected. Copy said Talkspace would find a licensed provider in that state and does not offer psychiatry outside the US.

required_inputs: Select how to pay
optional_inputs: Organization or EAP coverage option available
safe_stopping_point: Stop before submitting real insurance details or payment
main_strength: Strong insurance and EAP coverage path visibility
main_friction: Insurance dropdown and payer selection happen before provider visibility
legion_comparison: Legion shows providers and slots before payment. Talkspace pushes payment and coverage path earlier before specific provider visibility.
notes: Observed options included out-of-pocket, Anthem Blue Cross and Blue Shield, UnitedHealthcare, Aetna Medical Plans, and organization or EAP coverage.

required_inputs: Enter member ID, optional group ID, name, DOB, phone, address, organization details, referral source, email, and eligibility attestation
safe_stopping_point: Stop before using real insurance information
main_strength: Attempts to verify coverage before plan selection
main_friction: Very high data-entry burden before provider or booking visibility
legion_comparison: Legion's observed flow showed provider match before this level of personal and insurance-detail collection. Talkspace asks for much more before showing clinician choice.
notes: A coverage verification failure was observed. Talkspace offered continue without insurance, check information and resubmit, employer/EAP coverage, or claim plan covers Talkspace.

required_inputs: Choose psychiatry plan
insurance_or_payment_step: Self-pay plan selection after coverage failure
provider_visibility: No provider list observed before checkout
booking_visibility: No appointment slot observed before checkout
payment_point: Checkout comes before visible provider list in observed flow
safe_stopping_point: Stop before checkout or payment
main_strength: Clear plan options and clear statement that user will not be charged until matched
main_friction: No visible provider list or appointment-slot selection before plan checkout in the observed flow
legion_comparison: Legion is stronger on early provider visibility and slot visibility. Talkspace is stronger on packaged plan clarity but weaker on clinician transparency before checkout.
notes: Observed psychiatry plan options included initial evaluation, initial evaluation plus follow-up, and initial evaluation plus three follow-ups. Page stated user would not be charged until matched with a provider.

required_inputs: Click get started
insurance_or_payment_step: No insurance or payment submitted yet
safe_stopping_point: Continue before entering account or insurance data
main_strength: Excellent upfront explanation of the process
main_friction: Still starts before any provider or availability visibility
legion_comparison: Stronger than Legion on expectation-setting. Legion jumps faster into intake but explains the full process less clearly.
notes: Welcome screen explained three phases: insurance and location check, clinical matching questions, and book first visit.

required_inputs: Enter email and agree to terms
insurance_or_payment_step: No insurance or payment submitted yet
safe_stopping_point: Stop before using private email if needed
main_strength: Early account capture and continuation ability
main_friction: Email verification happens very early before provider visibility
legion_comparison: Legion created an account later in the observed flow. Talkiatry creates identity friction earlier.
notes: Observed authorization-code email verification screen and terms agreement checkbox.

required_inputs: Select care is for self or someone under 18
insurance_or_payment_step: No insurance or payment submitted yet
safe_stopping_point: Continue before deeper personal details
main_strength: Supports adult and minor pathways
main_friction: Adds another routing step before provider visibility
legion_comparison: Legion's observed flow focused on adults and virtual care. Talkiatry explicitly supports self or under-18 care routing.
notes: Observed options were Myself and Someone else who is under 18.

required_inputs: Enter legal name, DOB, phone, sex on file with insurer, address, and referral source
optional_inputs: Middle name and address line 2 optional
safe_stopping_point: Stop before submitting real insurance details if not comfortable
main_strength: Collects insurance-matching data carefully
main_friction: Heavy personal-data burden before users see matches
legion_comparison: Legion shows provider match much earlier. Talkiatry likely improves insurance accuracy but adds activation friction.
notes: Screen copy emphasized matching insurer records to verify coverage quickly and accurately.

required_inputs: Enter policyholder relationship and coverage details after verification failure
optional_inputs: Policyholder relationship choices include Me, Spouse, Parent, Stepparent, Foster parent, Grandparent, Other
booking_visibility: User can still book while coverage is being confirmed
safe_stopping_point: Continue without completing real booking
main_strength: Strong fallback because user can still book while insurer is contacted
main_friction: Coverage uncertainty remains unresolved before booking
legion_comparison: Legion allows provider visibility without this much insurance verification. Talkiatry's fallback is operationally strong but adds complexity.
notes: Observed message said coverage could not be verified and Talkiatry would contact insurer directly. It also said user could still book a visit in the meantime.

required_inputs: Choose all applicable reasons
safe_stopping_point: Continue before final booking
main_strength: Multi-select clinical routing captures comorbid needs well
main_friction: Longer clinical intake before provider visibility
legion_comparison: Similar to Legion's multi-select condition capture, but Talkiatry asks more clinically detailed questions before showing providers.
notes: Observed options included ADHD, Anxiety, Bipolar disorder, Depression, OCD, Postpartum depression, PTSD, and I'm not sure.

required_inputs: Answer questions about eating disorder, personality disorder, schizophrenia, self-harm, substance use, LGBTQIA+ care, pregnancy, hospitalization, suicide risk, alcohol, drugs, medication openness, emergency contact, and existing appointment status
provider_visibility: No provider shown until after screening
booking_visibility: No booking shown until after screening
safe_stopping_point: Stop before confirming appointment
main_strength: Strong clinical and safety discipline before matching
main_friction: Very long onboarding before provider list
legion_comparison: Legion is faster to provider cards. Talkiatry is deeper clinically and likely safer for matching, but heavier for activation.
notes: Observed extensive safety and clinical matching questions including self-harm risk, substance use, emergency contact, and medication openness.

required_inputs: Review matched clinicians
provider_visibility: Provider list shown with multiple clinicians
booking_visibility: Booking buttons shown on each clinician card
payment_point: No payment shown before observed booking modal
safe_stopping_point: Stop before confirming appointment
main_strength: Strong provider transparency and patient choice
main_friction: User reaches this only after long intake
legion_comparison: Talkiatry is strongest competitor against Legion for provider-choice experience. Legion shows provider cards earlier, but Talkiatry offers multiple clinician choices after deeper screening.
notes: Observed message: There are 8 clinicians who seem to be a great match for you. Provider cards showed name, credentials, specialty tags, bio link, earliest availability, and Book visit button.

required_inputs: Pick appointment day and time
payment_point: Confirm appointment button shown after selecting time
safe_stopping_point: Stop before confirming appointment
main_strength: Clear appointment scheduling after choosing clinician
main_friction: Calendar appears after clicking into one provider rather than in a full side-by-side comparison view
legion_comparison: Legion shows appointment times directly on provider cards, which is faster. Talkiatry's modal gives calendar control after clinician selection.
notes: Observed calendar for June 2026 with time slots such as 09:00 AM, 01:00 PM, 01:30 PM, 02:00 PM, 02:30 PM, 03:00 PM, and 03:30 PM.

required_inputs: Select who care is for, state, and insurance
safe_stopping_point: Continue before deeper clinical assessment
main_strength: Combines simple setup fields into one screen, reducing click count
main_friction: Insurance appears before user understands provider availability or specific cost
legion_comparison: More efficient than Talkspace's separate simple-detail screens. Legion's insurance step is separate but reaches provider cards faster.
notes: Observed first screen asked who care is for, state, and insurance together.

required_inputs: Select conditions and service type
safe_stopping_point: Continue before account or payment
main_strength: Multi-select condition capture and therapy/psychiatry selection are strong
main_friction: Broad list may feel long but captures complexity well
legion_comparison: Similar to Legion on multi-select conditions. Brightside adds explicit therapy versus psychiatry interest earlier.
notes: Observed condition list included depression, anxiety, insomnia, panic, bipolar disorder, OCD, PTSD, social anxiety, phobia, postpartum depression, burnout, binge eating/eating disorders, ADD/ADHD, substance or alcohol use issues, persistent depressive disorder, SAD, PMDD, relationship issues, borderline personality disorder, grief/loss, gambling issues, body image/weight issues, trauma, perimenopause-related issues, something else, and I'm not sure.

required_inputs: Answer PHQ/GAD-style symptom questions
safe_stopping_point: Continue before account or treatment selection
main_strength: Strong clinical assessment depth and measurement
main_friction: Long questionnaire before provider visibility
legion_comparison: Legion is faster and less burdensome. Brightside is stronger at making the intake feel clinically grounded.
notes: Observed repeated two-week symptom questions for depression and anxiety, functional difficulty, prior treatment, and recent ER or inpatient history.

required_inputs: Answer self-harm severity question if triggered
safe_stopping_point: Stop if any real crisis situation exists
main_strength: Strong safety handling and crisis routing
main_friction: Can make the flow emotionally intense, but this is clinically appropriate
legion_comparison: Brightside appears stronger than Legion on visible crisis-resource handling and suicide-prevention program routing in the observed flow.
notes: Observed crisis resources included hospital, 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline, 911, and Crisis Text Line 741-741. Brightside said user may be eligible for Suicide Prevention with Psychiatry program.

required_inputs: Create free account with first name and email
optional_inputs: Continue with external login option observed
safe_stopping_point: Stop before submitting sensitive or payment details
main_strength: Account creation protects results and enables continuation
main_friction: Account required before results and treatment options
legion_comparison: Legion creates account during flow as well. Brightside uses account creation before displaying assessment result feedback.
notes: Observed create free account screen after clinical assessment.

required_inputs: Review depression and anxiety scores
booking_visibility: Appointment availability message shown but no provider slot list observed
safe_stopping_point: Stop before choosing plan or entering payment
main_strength: Best observed result-feedback experience among competitors
main_friction: Still no specific provider list before the observed stopping point
legion_comparison: Brightside is stronger than Legion on clinical explanation and score feedback. Legion is stronger on early provider cards and slot specificity.
notes: Observed result screen showed mild depression 9/27, minimal anxiety 4/21, clinical level, and appointments available as soon as Wed Jun 24 at 8:00 AM. Next steps included mental health assessment, choose a plan, personal information, book appointments, and signup complete.

required_inputs: Enter primary insurance holder, legal name, DOB, and other coverage details
booking_visibility: No booking observed before plan/personal info path
payment_point: Payment not reached in observed flow
safe_stopping_point: Stop before entering real payment or final booking
main_strength: Structured insurance capture after assessment result
main_friction: Personal and insurance detail burden still appears before provider bios or booking
legion_comparison: Legion shows provider cards earlier. Brightside asks for more structured clinical and insurance data before specific provider visibility.
notes: Observed primary insurance holder screen with legal first name, legal last name, and DOB fields.
